notes: Each ignored vulnerability MUST be matched by a TODO row in requirements.txt explaining why the bump is deferred and what blocks it (typically: breaking-change scope).
- fastapi==0.109.1 (CVE-2024-47874, CVE-2025-54121 in starlette) — bump to 0.115+ requires Pydantic v2 lifecycle + route signature changes. Tracked: requirements.txt L17-20.
- cryptography==43.0.1 (CVE-2024-12797, CVE-2026-26007, CVE-2026-34073) — bump to 46.0.6 requires asyncpg/jwt/TLS validation. Tracked: requirements.txt L34-37.
- pytest==7.4.4 (CVE-2025-71176) — major bump to 9.x risks breaking conftest/plugins. Tracked: requirements.txt L49-50.

The ignored_ids list above is intentionally seeded with placeholder GHSA / PYSEC identifiers. On the first CI run after this lands, pip-audit will report the actual CVE/PYSEC/GHSA identifiers for these CVEs — the team should then replace the seeds with the exact ones reported. Until then, ANY new vulnerability will fail CI because it won't match these placeholders.
Process to add a new entry:
1. Add the vuln ID to ignored_ids.
2. Add a TODO row in requirements.txt with the bump-blocker reason.
3. Tag the entry above with the package + CVE in plain text.
